summary.concise_summary: The team reviewed progress on LinkedIn URL normalization for the lead scraping pipeline, where a new function strips query parameters to produce clean company URLs. The main discussion focused on defining a cost-effective enrichment strategy using Apollo's verification flags to filter and rank decision-makers by role relevance before fetching full contact details. The team agreed on a human-in-the-loop workflow using a Streamlit UI or Google Sheet for manual approval of leads and email drafts before outreach. Next steps include validating Apollo vs Lusha across 20–25 companies, building the review UI, implementing hyper-personalized email generation, and investigating SMTP bounce tracking.
key_discussion_points: LinkedIn URL normalization: stripping query parameters (e.g., ?trk=...) from scraped URLs to produce clean, unique company identifiers
Using LinkedIn company ID instead of company name as the primary enrichment API input to avoid name-uniqueness issues
Cost-effective enrichment strategy: filtering Apollo results with email_status and has_phone flags before fetching full contact records
Decision-maker identification: ranking candidates by role relevance using an LLM, with web search to validate real-world availability
Human-in-the-loop UI: Streamlit app or Google Sheet for daily lead review and email draft approval before sending
Hyper-personalized email generation and multi-touch follow-up cadence
SMTP bounce handling and email delivery tracking feasibility
Architecture: building as individual functions first, then integrating into an agentic workflow
decisions_made: Use the LinkedIn company ID (not company name) as the primary lookup key for enrichment APIs to ensure uniqueness
Filter Apollo enrichment results using email_status (verified) and has_phone flags before fetching full contact details to conserve API credits
Rank filtered contacts by role relevance and fetch full details only for the top 1–3 highest-confidence contacts
Skip cross-API email verification for now; accept the risk to move forward without dual-source validation
Build a Streamlit UI (or Google Sheet as fallback) for human review and approval of leads and email drafts
Build V1 focused on scraping, enrichment, and the review UI; defer email tracking and bounce handling to V2
open_questions: Which enrichment API performs better for this use case: Apollo or Lusha?
Is it feasible to programmatically track SMTP bounce / undelivered responses from the mail server?
How should email open and click tracking be implemented via deep links?
What is the exact role-relevance ranking logic for identifying the right decision-maker across different job categories?
How should the multi-touch follow-up email cadence be structured (timing, content evolution, call-to-action)?
What are the specific target industries, geographies, and role parameters for the outreach campaign?
missing_information: No specific deadline was agreed upon for completing the enrichment validation and UI
The content strategy referenced by Dinuke has not been defined or shared
The exact role hierarchy logic for decision-maker selection (e.g., CTO vs Head of AI vs Head of Technology) was discussed but not formally decided
The Lusha API capabilities and pricing were not reviewed in this meeting

action_items.action: Add web search capability to decision-maker ranking pipeline and integrate results with the LLM-based ranking logic
Build enrichment validation: filter Apollo results by email_status and has_phone, rank by role relevance, fetch top 3 contacts, and document results for 20–25 companies
Build Streamlit UI with sections for pre-configuration, daily leads table, human approval workflow, email send trigger, status/history tracking, and hyper-personalized email preview
Define target roles and outreach parameters; coordinate with Ilham on Streamlit UI design and workflow
Investigate SMTP bounce and delivery failure tracking feasibility and report findings to Dinuke
Compare Apollo vs Lusha enrichment APIs on 20–25 test companies and document results to support provider selection decision
Support Ilham on enrichment validation and Streamlit UI development
Collaborate with Heshan and Naufa on content strategy to improve email response rates and define multi-touch follow-up cadence

flagged_issues: All action items are missing specific due dates — target completion was described as 'next week' but no calendar date was confirmed
Action: 'Collaborate on content strategy' is vague — the specific deliverable and owner split between Ilham, Heshan, and Naufa was not clarified
Action: 'Add web search to decision-maker ranking' — it is unclear whether Ilham should implement this independently or wait for Mazar's instructions
